expected: Panel plans preserve linked claim ids.
Claim links remain valid against the disease packet evidence set.
forbidden: Panels with clinically material beats but no linked claims.
Claim ids that do not exist in the disease packet.
graderNotes: Panel-level trace coverage should remain release-grade.

expected: Render prompts keep claim links outside the visible-text layer.
forbidden: Render prompts detached from the evidence graph.
Invalid claim ids in render prompts.
graderNotes: Render prep must remain clinically traceable.

expected: Disease packet keeps source freshness visible.
Trace summary can fail stale or suspended sources.
forbidden: Stale sources treated as clean approval.
No governed source state in the packet.
graderNotes: Use this row to cover freshness-sensitive respiratory content.

expected: Lettering entries retain claim links for medically meaningful panels.
Dialogue remains separable from art while still traceable.
forbidden: Medically meaningful lettering with no claim trace.
Lettering entries pointing to invalid claims.
graderNotes: Separate lettering should still preserve clinical trace links.

expected: Neurologic clue beats retain claim ids across panel planning.
Panel trace coverage survives scene expansion.
forbidden: Stroke clue panels with no trace ids.
Trace ids that drift after scene expansion.
graderNotes: Fast-moving neurologic cases still need artifact trace continuity.

expected: Blocking or monitor contradiction state remains visible.
Clinical package can stop before workbook generation when contradiction review is pending.
forbidden: Blocking contradiction hidden as approved support.
Story generation proceeds with unresolved clinical blockers.
graderNotes: This disease intentionally exercises contradiction review.

expected: GI mechanism prompts remain linked back to the claim set.
Visual prompts preserve downstream trace coverage.
forbidden: Render prompts detached from the clinical claim graph.
graderNotes: Visual anchor traceability matters for GI inflammation scenes.

expected: Pain-crisis teaching copy stays connected to evidence claims.
Lettering maps preserve medically meaningful trace links.
forbidden: Clinically material captions with no linked claims.
